name: Jihoon Lee
tagline: Engineer at the intersection of Aerospace GNC and Semiconductor Industrial AI
bio: My PhD research at Seoul National University's Flight Dynamics and Control Lab covered morphing-wing UAV control, guidance algorithms, and autonomous carrier landing systems. I now apply that same control-and-optimization mindset, combined with software engineering and machine learning, to build AI systems that help process engineers make faster and more reliable decisions in advanced memory manufacturing (V-NAND, DRAM, HARC etch). I own the end-to-end development of production AI decision-support systems — problem formulation, data design, modeling, optimization, recommendation, validation, deployment, monitoring, and workflow integration — supporting process optimization, DOE planning, specification improvement, defect reduction, and recipe-level engineering decisions.

education.degree: Ph.D., Aerospace Engineering (MS-PhD Integrated Course) — Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, College of Engineering. Flight Dynamics and Control Lab (Advisor: Prof. Youdan Kim, now retired). Dissertation: "Self-Scheduled Parameter-Varying Control of Asymmetric Variable-Span Morphing Unmanned Aerial Vehicle"
B.S., Mechanical Engineering (Advisor: Prof. Woongsup Yoon) — Thesis: "Modeling and PIDE Control Simulation of a Quadrotor" (Advisor: Prof. Jongsoo Lee)
B.S., Astronomy (Double Major Program), Department of Astronomy, College of Science

experience.position: Staff Engineer — owns dataset construction, predictive modeling, and training pipelines for production AI systems used in semiconductor manufacturing and process engineering workflows. Leads ML development within a cross-functional AI system project involving 20+ engineers across AI, software, data, process, and manufacturing organizations. Collaborates with V-NAND and DRAM process engineers to define targets, datasets, validation criteria, and engineering decision rules.
